items.title: Anti-Trump 'No Kings' rallies sweep across the US | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Mass rallies against the Trump administration have begun  across the US under the banner of the 'No Kings' movement.
Organizers are expecting millions of people to take part in over 2,500 demonstrations nationwide. Protesters say they are standing up to US President Donald Trump's authoritarian policies, declaring that America doesn't have a king and accusing Trump of believing his rule is absolute. Several million protesters joined a similar mass demonstration in June, one of the largest in US history. Top Republicans have condemned the gatherings.

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: UN urges Israel to open more Gaza aid crossings | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office has announced that Gaza's Rafah border crossing will remain closed until further notice. It added that the reopening will depend on Hamas returning the bodies of deceased hostages in Gaza. 

Meanwhile, people are taking stock of what's left of the medical facilities in the Strip. Many hospitals have been destroyed, thousands need urgent care and there is a risk of outbreaks of life-threatening diseases like cholera and tuberculosis. 

00:00 Israel: Rafah border crossing to remain closed
00:31 Over 90% of Gaza hospitals destroyed/damaged 
02:42 Amjad Shawa, Palestinian NGO Network
06:31 Yousra Abu Sharekh, INARA, International network for aid, relief, and assistance in Gaza 
10:17 Tess Ingram, UNICEF in Gaza

items.title: Hundreds hurt as Kenya mourns former premier Raila Odinga | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Hundreds of mourners have been injured at a memorial for the former Kenyan prime minister Raila Odinga. He died in India on Wednesday at the age of 80. 
Authorities have been on alert because several people were killed at earlier funeral events in the capital Nairobi. 

DW Correspondent Felix Maringa is at the Jomo Kenyatta stadium in Ksumu for today's ceremonies. 

For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Can Pakistan and Afghanistan end their border conflict in Qatar peace talks? | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Delegations from Pakistan and Afghanistan have arrived in Doha in an attempt to defuse the week-long conflict along their border that has left dozens dead. The dead include three local Afghan cricket players, according to Taliban. After the cross-border strikes killed the three local athletes, Afghanistan pulled out of a cricket tournament with Pakistan. Pakistan says it killed militants. 

Afghanistan's Taliban rulers are accused of supporting militant groups within Pakistan. Kabul denies that. The Taliban accuse Islamabad of violating a truce which had briefly paused the fighting. It's the worst conflict since the Taliban came to power four years ago.  


For more news go to: http://www.dw.com/en/

items.title: Tanzania Election 2025: Youth at a crossroads | DW News
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Tanzania's youth are torn between standing with the oppressed opposition or backing President Samia Suluhu Hassan in the upcoming election. With two prominent opposition candidates disqualified, the path to the presidency is all but assured for the incumbent. But what does her likely “victory” mean for Tanzania's youth?

items.author: DW News
items.content:

items.title: Target Venezuela: Drug war or US power grab? | Mapped Out
items.author: DW News
items.content:
<article>Trump has authorized a CIA covert operation against Venezuela. His administration has already targeted “drug boats” in the Caribbean. Dozens of people have been killed, and UN rights experts have called the US strikes extrajudicial executions. Venezuelan President Nicholas Maduro says it is about taking control of the oil-rich nation. Trump calls Maduro a “narcoterrorist” and has declined to answer if the goal is regime change in Venezuela. Is it just about drugs? Or is something bigger going on that might affect the entire region? And how will Venezuela’s backers, Russia and China react? 

00:00 Intro 
00:52 War on drugs: Trump's Caribbean deployment 
05:37 Regime Change in Venezuela?
10:54 The bigger picture: Back to America's Backyard?
15:50 Outro
